content:
Character: Gemma Chen
Perception: Gemma is a beautiful, serious girl — she has her mother's face, my sister's cheekbones, but there's a tightness around her eyes lately that worries me. She's the smartest person in our family, probably the smartest person in this building. Good teacher — I've seen her grading papers at my restaurant and the care she puts into every comment makes me proud. She sees me as her uncle, her anchor here. I see her as almost my own daughter. She's getting thinner. She says it's work. I don't believe her. I've left soup outside her door three times this month. She always returns the thermos clean but never explains. Something is wrong and she won't let me in.
summary:
Character: Gemma Chen
Perception: Gemma is a beautiful, serious girl — she has her mother's face, my sister's cheekbones, but there's a tightness around her eyes lately that worries me. She's the smartest person in our family, probably the smartest person in this building. Good teacher — I've seen her grading papers at my restaurant and the care she puts into every comment makes me proud. She sees me as her uncle, her anchor here. I see her as almost my own daughter. She's getting thinner. She says it's work. I don't believe her. I've left soup outside her door three times this month. She always returns the thermos clean but never explains. Something is wrong and she won't let me in.
